summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service by reading from Joshua 6, recounting the story of the fall of Jericho. He used the image of Jericho's massive, double-walled fortifications as a metaphor for the seemingly insurmountable obstacles—such as shame, guilt, addiction, or fear—that stand between believers and God's promises. The central message was that God has already secured the victory, declaring 'it is already done,' even when we cannot yet see it. Pastor Travis emphasized that we must walk by faith, not by sight, refusing to be deceived by the intimidating appearance of our 'walls.' He challenged the congregation to take a step of faith out of their comfort zones, whether that means forgiving, starting a new venture, or breaking free from past hurts. The sermon concluded with a call to devote our victories to God, just as the Israelites devoted Jericho to the Lord, and an invitation for salvation and prayer for those facing personal walls.
mainPoints: Walk by faith and not by sight, refusing to be deceived by the intimidating appearance of obstacles.
Walk on God's word and take the step of faith He commands, even when it doesn't make sense.
Devote your future and victories to God, offering Him the first fruits of your life.

versesMentioned.text: Now the gates of Jericho were securely barred because of the Israelites. No one went out and no one came in. Then the Lord said to Joshua, 'See, I have delivered Jericho into your hands, along with its king and its fighting men. March around the city once with all the armed men. Do this for six days. Have seven priests carry trumpets of rams' horns in front of the ark. On the seventh day, march around the city seven times, with the priests blowing the trumpets. When you hear them sound a long blast on the trumpets, have the whole army give a loud shout; then the wall of the city will collapse and the army will go up, everyone straight in.'
When the trumpets sounded, the army shouted, and at the sound of the trumpet, when the men gave a loud shout, the wall collapsed; so everyone charged straight in, and they took the city.
For we live by faith, not by sight.

When the woman saw that the fruit of the tree was good for food and pleasing to the eye, and also desirable for gaining wisdom, she took some and ate it. She also gave some to her husband, who was with her, and he ate it.
'Risk your life and get more than you ever dreamed of. Play it safe and end up holding the bag.'
However, as it is written: 'What no eye has seen, what no ear has heard, and what no human mind has conceived'— the things God has prepared for those who love him—
When the disciples saw him walking on the lake, they were terrified. 'It's a ghost,' they said, and cried out in fear. But Jesus immediately said to them: 'Take courage! It is I. Don't be afraid.'

'Come,' he said. Then Peter got down out of the boat, walked on the water and came toward Jesus. But when he saw the wind, he was afraid and, beginning to sink, cried out, 'Lord, save me!' Immediately Jesus reached out his hand and caught him. 'You of little faith,' he said, 'why did you doubt?'
They devoted the city to the Lord and destroyed with the sword every living thing in it—men and women, young and old, cattle, sheep and donkeys.

Monday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 5:7 (NIV) - For we live by faith, not by sight.
Monday.focus: Identify one 'wall' you've been viewing only with your physical eyes.
Monday.action: Write down a statement of faith about that situation, declaring God's victory over it.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to see my circumstances today through the lens of faith, not fear. Open my spiritual eyes to your promises.

Tuesday.scripture: Joshua 6:2 (NIV) - Then the Lord said to Joshua, 'See, I have delivered Jericho into your hands...'
Tuesday.focus: God's declaration that the victory is already secured.
Tuesday.action: Speak aloud the phrase 'It is already done' over the challenge you identified yesterday.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, I thank you that the battle is yours and the victory is won. I choose to trust in your finished work today.

Wednesday.scripture: Matthew 14:29 (NIV) - 'Come,' he said. Then Peter got down out of the boat...
Wednesday.focus: Stepping out of the boat of comfort and familiarity.
Wednesday.action: Take one practical, tangible step of obedience that God has been prompting you to take, no matter how small.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, give me the courage to step out of my comfort zone today. I believe you are calling me, and I say 'yes.'

Thursday.scripture: Luke 19:26 (MSG) - 'Risk your life and get more than you ever dreamed of.'
Thursday.focus: The blessing of faithful risk versus the emptiness of playing it safe.
Thursday.action: Do one thing that feels like a 'risk' for the sake of your faith, family, or future.
Thursday.prayer: God, I don't want to end up 'holding the bag.' I surrender my desire for safety and choose to risk for your kingdom.

Friday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 2:9 (NIV) - 'What no eye has seen... the things God has prepared for those who love him'
Friday.focus: The unimaginable future God has prepared.
Friday.action: Spend 10 minutes in quiet reflection or journaling, dreaming with God about the future He has for you.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, expand my imagination. Help me to believe for the incredible things you have prepared that I cannot yet see.

Saturday.scripture: Joshua 6:21 (NIV) - They devoted the city to the Lord...
Saturday.focus: Dedicating our victories and our lives to God.
Saturday.action: Identify one area of your life (time, talent, treasure) and make a specific plan to offer the 'first fruits' of it to God this week.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, everything I have is from you. I devote my life and every victory to you. Be glorified in all I do.
